source: WhatsApp course chat analysis — דימה (repeat student) + class discussion

items.text: Proofs only from lectures — Asaf only asks to reproduce proofs he actually demonstrated in class. If he didn't prove it, it won't be an exam proof question.
Not taught ≠ not on exam — However, technical questions (not proofs) can come from any part of the textbook material.
Strong Duality was NOT proved this semester → will NOT appear as a proof question.
Shadow prices (מחיר מתואם) were NOT taught → will NOT appear.
Farkas Lemma was NOT taught → will NOT appear.
Mixing Lemma & Expansion Lemma were NOT proved → will NOT appear as proof questions.
Fibonacci logarithmic bound — "every semester he proves it and every other exam it appears" — study even though he only partially covered it this semester.
Asaf recycles questions — even year to year, same exact questions can appear.
Moed B is harder — Asaf explicitly said this in the last lecture.
Formula sheet provided — standard formulas are given, but you need to know what to do with them.
Vertex definitions — 2025A CONFIRMED דימה's suspicion: polytope geometry appeared as Q2 in Exam 2! When דימה flags something as 'suspicious,' TRUST HIM.
One 'hard' question — there's usually one 29-point question that's very challenging and differentiates students.

2025A Trend: topics ABSENT from 2025A (Dynamic Lists, BPP, Eigenvalues, LP Approximation) are now 'due' for 2026. Topics TESTED in 2025A (Decrease-Key, LP Duality defs) are less likely to repeat verbatim.
